title: rubus gratus subsp. latifolius
intro:
Rubus gratus subsp. latifolius, also known as the broad-leaved thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the thimbleberry plant, a type of shrub native to North America, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, attributed to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Rubus gratus subsp. latifolius include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
